questions.text: Which of these is not a valid access modifier in Java?
questions.explanation: Java supports four access levels: private, package-private (no modifier), protected, and public. "internal" is a keyword in Kotlin and C#, but not in Java.

questions.text: What is the size of double variable in Java?
questions.explanation: In Java, the double data type is a double-precision 64-bit IEEE 754 floating point number.

questions.text: Which class is the superclass of all classes in Java?
questions.explanation: Class Object is the root of the class hierarchy in Java. Every class has Object as a superclass. All objects, including arrays, implement the methods of this class.

questions.text: Which of the following is an immutable data type in Python?
questions.explanation: Tuples are immutable in Python, meaning once they are created, their elements cannot be changed or re-assigned.

questions.text: What does the "self" keyword represent in Python class methods?
questions.explanation: "self" represents the specific instance of the class being operated on, allowing access to attributes and methods of that instance.

questions.text: Which function is used to get the length of a list in Python?
questions.explanation: The built-in len() function returns the number of items in an object, such as a list, string, tuple, or dictionary.

questions.text: What does the "C" in ACID properties of transaction stand for?
questions.explanation: ACID stands for Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability. Consistency ensures that a transaction takes the database from one valid state to another.

questions.text: Which normal form is concerned with multi-valued dependencies?
questions.explanation: Fourth Normal Form (4NF) is violated when there are one or more multi-valued dependencies. Eliminating these results in 4NF.

questions.text: What is a foreign key used for?
options: To unique identify rows in the current table
To establish a relationship between two tables
To encrypt columns of sensitive data
questions.explanation: A foreign key is a column or group of columns in one table that references the primary key in another table, creating an active link between them.

questions.text: Which SQL keyword is used to sort the result-set?
questions.explanation: The ORDER BY keyword is used to sort the result-set in ascending or descending order.

questions.text: Which join returns all rows when there is a match in either left or right table?
questions.explanation: FULL OUTER JOIN returns all matching and non-matching rows from both the left and right tables.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the SQL HAVING clause?
options: To filter groups after the GROUP BY clause is applied
questions.explanation: The HAVING clause was added to SQL because the WHERE keyword could not be used with aggregate functions. It filters grouped records.

questions.text: Which HTML5 element is used to represent self-contained, independent article content?
questions.explanation: The <article> tag specifies independent, self-contained content. An article should make sense on its own and should be distributable.

questions.text: How do you create an email link in HTML?
questions.explanation: The "mailto:" scheme is placed in the href attribute of an <a> element to prompt the browser to open an email composer.

questions.text: Which attribute specifies an alternate text for an image if the image cannot be displayed?
questions.explanation: The required alt attribute provides alternative text for an image, vital for screen readers and search engines.

questions.text: Which CSS property is used to control the layout alignment of elements inside a Flexbox container?
questions.explanation: The align-items property specifies the default alignment for items inside the flexible container along the cross axis.

questions.text: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets, defining how HTML elements are to be displayed on screen, paper, or in other media.

questions.text: How do you select an element with id "demo" in CSS?
questions.explanation: The hash (#) symbol is used in CSS selectors to match elements with a matching id attribute value.

questions.text: What is the output of "typeof null" in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: This is a long-standing legacy bug in JavaScript where null is treated as an object type in type inspections.

questions.text: Which keyword is used to declare block-scoped variables in modern JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The let and const keywords declare variables that are local to the enclosing block, statement, or expression.

questions.text: Which method returns a new array with elements that pass a testing function?
questions.explanation: The filter() method creates a shallow copy of a portion of a given array, filtered down to just the elements from the given array that pass the test.

questions.text: Which hook is used to perform side effects in functional React components?
questions.explanation: The useEffect Hook lets you perform side effects (such as data fetching, subscriptions, or manual DOM changes) in function components.

questions.text: What must be unique among sibling components in React list rendering?
questions.explanation: Keys help React identify which items have changed, are added, or are removed, ensuring stability and performance.

questions.text: Can a React component update its own props directly?
options: Only if props contain callback functions
questions.explanation: All React components must act like pure functions with respect to their props. Props are immutable and read-only from the component perspective.

questions.text: Which annotation is the combination of @Configuration, @EnableAutoConfiguration, and @ComponentScan?
questions.explanation: The @SpringBootApplication annotation is a convenience annotation that adds all three, booting up auto-configurations and local component scanning.

questions.text: Which port is the default embedded Tomcat server port in Spring Boot?
questions.explanation: By default, Spring Boot starts an embedded Apache Tomcat web server listening on port 8080.

questions.text: What does JPA stand for in Java Spring frameworks?
questions.explanation: JPA stands for Java Persistence API, which is a standard specification for Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) in Java.

questions.text: A car travels at 60 km/h for 2 hours and then 80 km/h for 3 hours. What is its average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Total distance = (60 * 2) + (80 * 3) = 120 + 240 = 360 km. Total time = 2 + 3 = 5 hours. Average speed = 360 / 5 = 72 km/h.

questions.text: Complete the number series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: The differences between consecutive terms are increasing by 2: (6-2)=4, (12-6)=6, (20-12)=8, (30-20)=10. The next difference is 12, so 30 + 12 = 42.

questions.text: If 5 men can complete a piece of work in 12 days, how many days will 10 men take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Using the work formula M1 * D1 = M2 * D2: 5 * 12 = 10 * D2 => 60 = 10 * D2 => D2 = 6 days.

questions.text: What operator is used to obtain the memory address of a variable in C?
questions.explanation: The address-of operator (&) returns the memory address of its operand variable.

questions.text: Which function is used for dynamic memory allocation in C?
questions.explanation: The malloc() function (memory allocation) allocates a specified block of memory in bytes and returns a void pointer to it.

questions.text: What is the format specifier used to print double variables in C using printf?
questions.explanation: "%lf" is the standard format specifier used to print double values in C, whereas "%f" is typically for single-precision float.
